title: acmella alpestris
intro: Acmella alpestris, also known as Paraherquemon alpestris, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of South America. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Acmella alpestris is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including pain relief, fever reduction, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or topical applications, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
